rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses product hedonism–utilitarianism as a focal moderator/boundary condition (Studies 3–4; effect stronger for utilitarian vs. hedonic products), while the cited 2000 JMKR paper is a core study on consumer choice between hedonic and utilitarian goods. Given this close construct overlap and the confirmed DOI match, the citation is plausibly a substantive theory/construct source rather than an incidental mention.
